lever_summary: Dense F64 axis (partial) reduction fast path. eval_reduce_axes' float branch decoded each input element's full multi-index (flat_to_multi_into + multi_to_out_flat) and matched as_f64() on the 24-byte Literal enum. New dense_f64_axis_reduce folds LiteralBuffer::as_f64_slice() directly using a row-major odometer that maintains the output flat index incrementally, removing both the per-element index decode (algorithmic) and the enum materialization (data-model).

alien_graveyard.primitive: vectorized execution / dense SoA layout + incremental index odometer (strength reduction of per-element address arithmetic)
alien_graveyard.mapping: replace tuple-at-a-time Literal decode and full multi-index recomputation with a contiguous f64 sweep whose destination index is advanced by a carry odometer (O(1) amortized per element)

method: Same-worker comparison on vmi1153651: the dense-storage fast path vs a Vec<Literal>-backed reference bench (eval/reduce_sum_256_axis{0,1}_f64_literal_ref) run in the same process, so the ratio isolates the lever from cross-worker CPU variance (the dense input previously hit the same generic decode loop after one-time Literal materialization).
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- reduce_sum_256_axis --save-baseline axisafter

change.lever: Added dense_f64_axis_reduce(tensor, kept_axes, out_dims, out_count, float_init, float_op) -> Option<Vec<f64>>; eval_reduce_axes' float branch tries it first and falls back to the unchanged generic loop when None (non-dense / non-F64).

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: the contiguous slice is visited in ascending flat order, identical to `for flat_idx in 0..total`; each output cell accumulates its inputs in the same sequence
isomorphism.index_mapping: the odometer's out_idx exactly reproduces multi_to_out_flat(flat_to_multi_into(flat)): out_axis_stride is row-major over out_dims ordered by kept_axes, reduced axes contribute 0, innermost axis varies fastest (input stride 1)
isomorphism.floating_point: unchanged: same float_init seed and caller float_op (ReduceSum +, ReduceProd *, ReduceMax jax_max_f64, ReduceMin jax_min_f64) applied sequentially; no reassociation or SIMD reduction tree
isomorphism.errors: as_f64_slice() is Some only for well-formed F64 dense storage (element count matches shape), so out_idx stays within 0..out_count and no malformed-input / overflow case reaches the fast path; complex, integral, F32/F16/BF16, and Literal-backed F64 inputs return None and run the unchanged generic loop; full-reduction (all axes) still delegates to eval_reduce before this branch
isomorphism.output_dtype: unchanged: reduce_real_output_dtype(F64) == F64; result built via the same reduce_real_literal mapping

validation.name: reduction_parity_and_unit_tests
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib reduction
validation.result: passed: 30 tests (incl. dense_f64_axis_reduce_bit_identical_to_literal_path: rank-3, all 4 reductions, 8 axis subsets, NaN/inf/signed-zero)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
